components.name: Windows Media Player (legacy)
components.description: Removes the classic Windows Media Player feature and capability. Separate from the modern Media Player app.
breaks: Audio CD ripping and playback have no in-box tool left
The wmplayer.exe path that some third-party apps shell out to no longer exists
WMV playback via the legacy player is gone, though the modern Media Player still handles most formats

components.name: Internet Explorer mode
components.description: Removes the Internet Explorer 11 optional component that backs Edge's IE mode.
breaks: Edge's IE mode stops working, so legacy intranet sites that need it will not load
Applications embedding the old MSHTML web control may fail to render
A few enterprise line-of-business apps that host IE fail to start

components.name: .NET Framework 3.5
components.description: Removes the .NET Framework 3.5 on-demand payload. It ships disabled, so this only removes the ability to turn it on offline.
breaks: Older applications targeting .NET 2.0 through 3.5 refuse to start and prompt to install a component that is no longer on the image
Enabling .NET 3.5 later needs an internet connection or the original media, not just the Optional Features dialog

components.name: XPS Services
components.description: Removes the XPS document services and the XPS Document Writer printer.

components.name: Microsoft Print to PDF
components.description: Removes the built-in Print to PDF virtual printer.
breaks: The Microsoft Print to PDF printer disappears, so there is no way to save any document as a PDF without third-party software
Apps offering a Save as PDF option that routes through the print subsystem lose it too

components.name: Hyper-V & Virtual Machine Platform
components.description: Removes the Hyper-V role, the hypervisor platform and the virtual machine platform that WSL 2 and Windows Sandbox build on.
breaks: WSL 2 cannot run at all — this breaks WSL even if you keep the WSL feature itself
Docker Desktop, Android emulators and Windows Sandbox stop working
Memory integrity and other virtualization-based security features cannot be enabled
Third-party hypervisors that rely on the Windows Hypervisor Platform API, including some VirtualBox and VMware configurations, lose hardware acceleration

components.name: Windows Subsystem for Linux
components.description: Removes the WSL optional feature. It ships disabled, so this removes the payload rather than turning anything off.
breaks: WSL cannot be enabled on the resulting image without adding the feature back from other media
wsl.exe is present but reports that the optional component is missing

components.name: PowerShell 2.0 engine
components.description: Removes the deprecated PowerShell 2.0 engine, which is a well-known downgrade-attack surface.

components.name: PowerShell ISE
components.description: Removes the PowerShell Integrated Scripting Environment.

components.name: Legacy Components & DirectPlay
components.description: Removes the legacy components feature and DirectPlay, the retired multiplayer API for pre-2008 games.
breaks: Games from roughly the XP era that use DirectPlay for multiplayer or copy protection fail to launch
The compatibility shim that normally offers to install DirectPlay on demand has nothing to install

components.name: Legacy Network Clients
components.description: Removes Telnet, TFTP, Simple TCP/IP services, SMB Direct, Work Folders and Remote Differential Compression. All ship disabled.

components.name: Remote Desktop
components.description: Blocks inbound Remote Desktop connections and removes the RemoteApp client capability. The mstsc client stays.
breaks: Nobody can connect to this machine over RDP, including you when it is headless
RemoteApp and Desktop Connections in Control Panel stops working
Remote Assistance and some remote management tooling that tunnels over the terminal service also stop

components.name: Steps Recorder
components.description: Removes Steps Recorder, which Microsoft has already deprecated.

components.name: OpenSSH Client
breaks: The ssh, scp and sftp commands are gone, which breaks Git over SSH and most remote shell workflows
Tools that shell out to ssh.exe — including VS Code Remote and Ansible — stop working until you install a replacement

components.name: Print Management Console
components.description: Removes the Print Management MMC snap-in. Ordinary printing is unaffected.
